title: Sort the People
content:
You are given an array of strings names, and an array heights that consists of distinct positive integers. Both arrays are of length n.

For each index i, names[i] and heights[i] denote the name and height of the ith person.

Return names sorted in descending order by the people's heights.

Example 1:

Input: names = ["Mary","John","Emma"], heights = [180,165,170]
Output: ["Mary","Emma","John"]
Explanation: Mary is the tallest, followed by Emma and John.

Example 2:

Input: names = ["Alice","Bob","Bob"], heights = [155,185,150]
Output: ["Bob","Alice","Bob"]
Explanation: The first Bob is the tallest, followed by Alice and the second Bob.

Constraints:

• n == names.length == heights.length

• 1 <= n <= 103

• 1 <= names[i].length <= 20

• 1 <= heights[i] <= 105

• names[i] consists of lower and upper case English letters.

• All the values of heights are distinct.

codeSnippets.code:
class Solution(object):
    def sortPeople(self, names, heights):
        """
        :type names: List[str]
        :type heights: List[int]
        :rtype: List[str]

hints: Find the tallest person and swap with the first person, then find the second tallest person and swap with the second person, etc. Repeat until you fix all n people.

solutions.explanation:
Zip names and heights. Sort by height descending. Extract names.
Time: O(n*log(n))
Space: O(n)
